Which of your study species, if any, would you be unwilling to share data about and why?
response: Regarding my previous answer, all the data was shared online, but we blurred their exact localisation. For all species, we blurred the localisation at a 400m grid cell. For two species, brown bear and capercaillie, we blurred the localisation at the mountain area level (centroid of a 800ha area). As those species are particularly sensitive (politically, in terms of their state of conservation, etc.), we found necessary to blur the exact location to prevent any disturbance by humans. If asked, we migh give the locations after discussing their use in detail with the person or project requesting them. For instance, I wouldn't give this information to someone who wants to go nature tourism or wildlife watching.

response: I have restrictions that are not species specific but related to the region and landowner. For some regions we have an embargo of one years on the data and an agreement that the animals sightings would be shared on a 10 by 10 km resolution only, this was requested by the landowners so we could install camera traps on their sites.
